# Example of running Go tests with scripttest
#
# This script shows how to use Go's testing framework within scripttest

# Create a temporary working directory for our test
cd ${WORK}

# Create a simple Go module
exec go mod init example.com/mcptest

# Create a function to test
cat > mcp_client.go << 'EOF'
package mcptest

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
)

// MCPClient represents a client for MCP servers
type MCPClient struct {
	ServerURL string
	NextID    int
}

// NewClient creates a new MCP client
func NewClient(serverURL string) *MCPClient {
	return &MCPClient{
		ServerURL: serverURL,
		NextID:    1,
	}
}

// Initialize connects to the server and performs initialization
func (c *MCPClient) Initialize() (map[string]interface{}, error) {
	request := map[string]interface{}{
		"jsonrpc": "2.0",
		"id":      c.NextID,
		"method":  "initialize",
		"params": map[string]interface{}{
			"protocolVersion": "2024-11-05",
			"capabilities":    map[string]interface{}{},
			"clientInfo": map[string]interface{}{
				"name":    "mcp-test-client",
				"version": "1.0.0",
			},
		},
	}
	c.NextID++
	
	return c.SendRequest(request)
}

// Write writes content to a file
func (c *MCPClient) Write(path, content string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
	request := map[string]interface{}{
		"jsonrpc": "2.0",
		"id":      c.NextID,
		"method":  "write",
		"params": map[string]interface{}{
			"path":    path,
			"content": content,
		},
	}
	c.NextID++
	
	return c.SendRequest(request)
}

// Read reads a file
func (c *MCPClient) Read(path string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
	request := map[string]interface{}{
		"jsonrpc": "2.0",
		"id":      c.NextID,
		"method":  "read",
		"params": map[string]interface{}{
			"path": path,
		},
	}
	c.NextID++
	
	return c.SendRequest(request)
}

// SendRequest sends a request to the MCP server
func (c *MCPClient) SendRequest(request map[string]interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
	// Marshal request to JSON
	requestJSON, err := json.Marshal(request)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error marshaling request: %w", err)
	}
	
	// Create HTTP request
	req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", c.ServerURL, bytes.NewBuffer(requestJSON))
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error creating request: %w", err)
	}
	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	
	// Send request
	client := &http.Client{}
	resp, err := client.Do(req)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error sending request: %w", err)
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()
	
	// Read response
	body, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response: %w", err)
	}
	
	// Parse response
	var response map[string]interface{}
	err = json.Unmarshal(body, &response)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing response: %w", err)
	}
	
	// Check for JSON-RPC error
	if errObj, ok := response["error"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
		errCode := 0
		if code, ok := errObj["code"].(float64); ok {
			errCode = int(code)
		}
		errMsg := "unknown error"
		if msg, ok := errObj["message"].(string); ok {
			errMsg = msg
		}
		return response, fmt.Errorf("MCP error %d: %s", errCode, errMsg)
	}
	
	return response, nil
}
EOF

# Create a Go test file that tests the client against a mock server
cat > mcp_client_test.go << 'EOF'
package mcptest

import (
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"
)

func TestMCPClient(t *testing.T) {
	// Start the scripttest server
	serverCmd := exec.Command("mcp-scripttest-server", 
		"-script", "server_script.txt", 
		"-port", "8085",
		"-workdir", "test_workdir")
	
	err := serverCmd.Start()
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Failed to start script server: %v", err)
	}
	
	// Ensure server is killed when test completes
	defer func() {
		serverCmd.Process.Kill()
		serverCmd.Wait()
	}()
	
	// Give the server time to start
	time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
	
	// Create client
	client := NewClient("http://localhost:8085/mcp")
	
	// Test initialization
	t.Run("Initialize", func(t *testing.T) {
		resp, err := client.Initialize()
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("Initialize failed: %v", err)
		}
		
		result, ok := resp["result"].(map[string]interface{})
		if !ok {
			t.Fatalf("Invalid response format")
		}
		
		serverInfo, ok := result["serverInfo"].(map[string]interface{})
		if !ok {
			t.Fatalf("Missing serverInfo")
		}
		
		name, ok := serverInfo["name"].(string)
		if !ok || name != "mcp-scripttest-server" {
			t.Errorf("Expected server name 'mcp-scripttest-server', got '%s'", name)
		}
	})
	
	// Test write operation
	t.Run("Write", func(t *testing.T) {
		resp, err := client.Write("test.txt", "Test content")
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("Write failed: %v", err)
		}
		
		result, ok := resp["result"].(map[string]interface{})
		if !ok {
			t.Fatalf("Invalid response format")
		}
		
		status, ok := result["status"].(string)
		if !ok || status != "success" {
			t.Errorf("Expected status 'success', got '%s'", status)
		}
	})
	
	// Test read operation
	t.Run("Read", func(t *testing.T) {
		resp, err := client.Read("test.txt")
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("Read failed: %v", err)
		}
		
		result, ok := resp["result"].(map[string]interface{})
		if !ok {
			t.Fatalf("Invalid response format")
		}
		
		content, ok := result["content"].(string)
		if !ok || content != "Test content" {
			t.Errorf("Expected content 'Test content', got '%s'", content)
		}
	})
	
	// Test read non-existent file
	t.Run("ReadNonExistent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := client.Read("nonexistent.txt")
		if err == nil {
			t.Fatal("Expected error when reading non-existent file")
		}
		
		if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), "File not found") {
			t.Errorf("Expected 'File not found' error, got: %v", err)
		}
	})
}
EOF

# Create the script for the mock server
mkdir -p test_workdir
cat > server_script.txt << 'EOF'
# Mock filesystem server for testing

# Initialize response
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize"}
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","serverInfo":{"name":"mcp-scripttest-server","version":"1.0.0"},"capabilities":{}}}

# Write file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"write","params":{"path":"*","content":"*"}}
exec mkdir -p $(dirname ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path})
exec echo "${REQUEST.params.content}" > ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"status":"success","path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","size":${#REQUEST.params.content},"message":"File written successfully"}}

# Read file
expect-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"read","params":{"path":"*"}}
# Check if the file exists
exec test -f ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path}
if ERRORLEVEL != 0
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"error":{"code":-32500,"message":"File not found: ${REQUEST.params.path}"}}
else
  set-var FILE_CONTENT = $(cat ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path})
  set-var FILE_SIZE = $(wc -c < ${WORKDIR}/${REQUEST.params.path} | tr -d ' ')
  send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":${ID},"result":{"path":"${REQUEST.params.path}","content":"${FILE_CONTENT}","size":${FILE_SIZE},"encoding":"utf-8"}}
endif
EOF

# Run the tests
! stdout "FAIL"
go test -v
stdout "=== RUN   TestMCPClient"
stdout "=== RUN   TestMCPClient/Initialize"
stdout "=== RUN   TestMCPClient/Write"
stdout "=== RUN   TestMCPClient/Read"
stdout "=== RUN   TestMCPClient/ReadNonExistent"
stdout "PASS"

# Check that the test actually created the file
exists test_workdir/test.txt
cat test_workdir/test.txt
stdout "Test content"
